exemplar.title: How do different types of sweeteners (such as natural sweeteners, artificial sweeteners, sugar alcohols, novel sweeteners and corn syrup) affect the rate of respiration in fermented Saccharomyces cerevisiae by using a carbon dioxide sensor?

detailedExemplarData.title: How do different types of sweeteners (such as natural sweeteners, artificial sweeteners, sugar alcohols, novel sweeteners and corn syrup) affect the rate of respiration in fermented Saccharomyces cerevisiae by using a carbon dioxide sensor?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question situated within a specific, authentic culinary and biochemical context
Methodological considerations are clearly explained with rationale for controls, sensor range, trial number and timing
Procedure description is sufficiently detailed to allow reproduction by another practitioner
weaknesses: Background lacks deeper linkage to yeast metabolic pathways and enzyme kinetics
Confounding variables such as pH and yeast age are omitted from the variables table
Minor ambiguities in procedure (typo in step numbering, unclear Tropicana Slim dilution) and unlabeled figures
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands in Research Design meet the band-5 descriptor with specific and explained context, methodological considerations, and reproducibility. Minor omissions (missing confounders, narrative digressions, small ambiguities) prevent a perfect band-6, so a holistic score of 5 is fully supported.

strengths: Data recording and processing is communicated clearly in places (readable tables, graphs, error bars)
Uncertainties are identified and one sample calculation is shown with error bars included
Data processing includes means, rates, standard deviations and a one-way ANOVA
weaknesses: Tables are inconsistently formatted, missing units and incomplete erythritol data
Uncertainty propagation to processed means is absent and some uncertainties are mis-expressed
Statistical labels and figure/table numbering are mislabeled and several graphs lack axis labels with units
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each strand of Data Analysis shows evidence of competence but with significant omissions or inaccuracies: communication is clear or precise (not both), uncertainties are considered but incompletely, and data processing has inconsistencies. These mid-band characteristics align with a score of 3.

strengths: Conclusion is relevant to the research question and largely consistent with the analysis
Integration and comparison with published fermentation work is well justified within the scientific context
weaknesses: Minor mismatches between stated ranking and plotted data reduce full consistency
Quantitative values and statistical thresholds are omitted from the conclusion
Occasional citation inaccuracies weaken absolute precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion fulfils the upper-mid band (3â4) for relevance and consistency with analysis, and meets the top band (5â6) for scientific context comparison by integrating literature. Small inconsistencies and omitted quantitative detail justify a holistic score of 5.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses are identified (sensor resolution, calibration, yeast strain, impurities) and their impacts are clearly explained
Proposed improvements are realistic, directly linked to identified limitations, and their effect on reliability and accuracy is explained
weaknesses: Feasibility and resource implications of the improvements are not discussed
Some potential limitations (e.g., pH drift monitoring) are not addressed
Depth on practical implementation is limited
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands in Evaluation meet the top-band descriptor by explaining the relative impact of specific limitations and linking improvements to those weaknesses. A lack of feasibility discussion and partial omission of other confounders keep this at a solid band-5 rather than 6.

strandSelections.reasoning: The conclusion summarises the ranking of sweeteners, references mean data and acknowledges that the hypothesis was rejected.  Quantitative values are mentioned, but minor mismatches (e.g. stating stevia has âsecond highestâ yet earlier graph shows corn-syrup similar) reduce full consistency.  Hence upper-middle band 4.

Complete raw data are provided for some sweeteners but tables are inconsistent (headers mis-aligned, missing units, truncated erythritol table).  Graphs and processed tables are readable, so communication is **clear OR precise**, not both; thus mid-band 3 is justified.

Means, rates, standard deviations and one-way ANOVA are calculated and largely appropriate, yet the rate formula stated is incorrect and some raw data sets are incomplete, casting doubt on accuracy.  These deficiencies indicate processing with significant inconsistencies â mid-band 3.

Specific weaknesses (sensor resolution, calibration, single-species yeast, impurities) are identified and their impacts on variability or validity are **explained** rather than merely listed, meeting the top-band descriptor.  One or two aspects (e.g. pH drift) are overlooked, so a solid 5 is awarded.

The student explicitly compares findings with published fermentation work (Pepin & Marzzacco) and links chemical structures to metabolic pathways, providing reasoned justification rooted in accepted context.  The comparison is integrated rather than superficial, satisfying the 5-6 band; occasional citation inaccuracies warrant 5 not 6.

The student clearly locates the question within a detailed and authentic baking/food-science scenario and links each selected sweetener to practical culinary usage and to its putative biochemical relevance.  This is a specific, appropriate and non-generic context, exceeding the âbroadâ requirement of the 3â4 band.  Minor narrative digressions prevent a perfect score, but the descriptor for 5-6 is substantially met.

Instrument uncertainties are listed and one sample calculation of relative/percentage uncertainty is shown; error bars are included on the comparative graph.  However, propagation to means/rates is absent and some quoted uncertainties are mis-expressed.  Evidence of consideration exists but with significant omissions, matching the 3â4 band (score 3).

Proposed improvements (gas syringe, multiple yeast strains, sensor calibration, crystalline corn-syrup) are directly linked to stated weaknesses and the student clarifies how each would raise reliability or accuracy, fulfilling the âexplainedâ criterion of 5-6.  Limited depth on feasibility means a 5 rather than 6.

A full variables table, rationale for each control, and discussion of confounding temperature demonstrate that the student has **explained** how relevant and sufficient data will be obtained.  Sensor range, trial number and timing are justified, showing more than a simple statement.  Some controls (e.g. pH, yeast age) are omitted, so a secure 5 rather than 6 is appropriate.

Step-by-step procedure lists precise masses, volumes, temperatures, apparatus and trial numbers; another practitioner could reproduce the work with minimal interpretation.  Ambiguities (typos in step numbering, unclear dilution for Tropicana Slim) keep it from flawless, but it still satisfies the âallows â¦ to be reproducedâ statement of the top band.

annotations.comment: Background information defines sweetener categories but lacks deeper critical linkage to yeast metabolic pathways; consider integrating enzyme kinetics or pathway diagrams.
Provide detailed calculation steps for corn syrup dilution in the variables table to avoid ambiguity in concentration preparation.
The materials table lists yeast but omits strain or age information; specify yeast batch details to enhance reproducibility.
Procedure illustrations lack axis labels and annotations; label each step clearly to guide the reader through Figure 2.
The raw data table for glucose shows missing erythritol data in the same format; ensure each sweetenerâs dataset is complete and uniformly formatted.
The research question is situated within a specific and appropriate context, linking each sweetenerâs culinary use to its biochemical impact on yeast COâ production.
The student demonstrates strong personal engagement, connecting a passion for baking with scientific inquiry into yeast respiration.
The description of anaerobic respiration ends abruptly; clarify the glycolysis and fermentation stages and ensure the figure legend fully explains all intermediates.
The independent variable table is truncated and missing complete explanations; extend the reasoning cell to detail the control of sweetener concentration.
The methodology section provides a clear, step-by-step procedure that would allow another researcher to reproduce the experiment.
The hypothesis outlines an expected order of COâ production but could be enhanced by specifying expected magnitude differences or statistical thresholds.
The controlled variables table omits confounding factors such as pH and yeast age; populate the confounding variables section accordingly.
The confounding variable table is blank; identify at least two potential confounders (e.g., pH drift, yeast viability) and their controlling methods.
Yeast activation step lacks temperature monitoring for the warm water; include a thermometer reading to ensure consistent rehydration conditions.
Percentage uncertainty for time intervals is missing the percentage symbol; correct to ââ0.067 %â to maintain clarity.
Figure 8 is labeled as a table of standard deviations; correct the numbering and ensure the caption reflects statistical content.
Qualitative observations are described but lack a systematic approach; consider coding bubble vigor and odor intensity for consistency.
The caption for Table 4.1 omits units for time and uncertainty; include âseconds (Â±0.2 s)â and âppm (Â±100 ppm)â explicitly.
The discussion of plateau in carbon dioxide production lacks reference to a threshold criterion; define how the plateau was determined.
Uncertainties in processed means are not propagated; apply uncertainty propagation from raw data to the mean COâ values.
Table 4.2 caption should indicate sample size (n = 5) and uncertainty sources; refine caption to reflect these details.
The text asserts a faster COâ plateau for stevia without matching data reference; verify against raw data or correct the interpretation.
Artificial sweetener data tables show identical values at later intervals; discuss potential sensor saturation or data truncation.
Figure 7.1 lacks axis labels with units (COâ ppm, time s); include clear labels and units to improve data communication.
The discussion implies non-overlapping error bars demonstrate significance; clarify that statistical tests (ANOVA) support this conclusion.
Proposed improvements are relevant but lack discussion of feasibility and resource implications; elaborate on practical implementation.
The null hypothesis notation in the ANOVA explanation is confusing; restate Hâ and decision rule more clearly.
The conclusion restates findings but omits key quantitative results; include mean COâ values and rates to justify the ranking.
The comparison to published work (Pepin & Marzzacco) is well integrated and supports the conclusion in the scientific context.
The evaluation identifies specific methodological weaknesses (sensor calibration, single yeast strain) and explains their impact on data variability.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
The research question is stated without context.
Methodological considerations associated with collecting data relevant to the research question are stated.
The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data lacks the detail to allow for the investigation to be reproduced.
The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced with few ambiguities or omissions.
The research question is outlined within a broad context.
Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are described.
The research question is described within a specific and appropriate context.
Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are explained.
The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
The recording and processing of the data is communicated but is neither clear nor precise.
Some processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with major omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.
The recording and processing of data shows limited evidence of the consideration of uncertainties.
The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with some significant omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.
The communication of the recording and processing of the data is either clear or precise.
The recording and processing of data shows evidence of a consideration of uncertainties but with some significant omissions or inaccuracies.
The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out appropriately and accurately.
The recording and processing of data shows evidence of an appropriate consideration of uncertainties.
The communication of the recording and processing of the data is both clear and precise.

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Communication of data recording and processing

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
The conclusion makes superficial comparison to the accepted scientific context.
A conclusion is stated that is relevant to the research question but is not supported by the analysis presented.
A conclusion is described that makes some relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.
A conclusion is described that is relevant to the research question but is not fully consistent with the analysis presented.
A conclusion is justified that is relevant to the research question and fully consistent with the analysis presented.
A conclusion is justified through relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
Realistic improvements to the investigation are stated.
The report states generic methodological weaknesses or limitations.
Realistic improvements to the investigation that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are described.
The report describes specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.
Realistic improvements to the investigation, that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are explained.
The report explains the relative impact of specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.
